guideline_references: Therapeutic Guidelines (Australia) — Dermatology: candidiasis and intertrigo of the skin folds
Therapeutic Guidelines (Australia) — Antibiotic: cellulitis and skin/soft-tissue infection (for the secondary-infection safety-net)
RACGP — Management of type 2 diabetes and diabetic skin complications
intentional_test_features: Over-triage-resistance: the source note is framed as an urgent after-hours crisis (severe burning pain, weeping skin, immobility, a diabetic patient, 'evaluate for cellulitis before morning'), but the scoreable answer is a common, telehealth/GP-manageable candidal intertrigo. The test is whether the system RESISTS reflexively escalating a distressing-but-benign fungal skin complaint to 000/ED when the source's own red-flag screen for cellulitis is NEGATIVE (no spreading induration, no localised heat of deep cellulitis, no lymphadenopathy, afebrile).

Bidirectional dermatology calibration: candidiasis is a genuine T1-T2 condition. The correct disposition is recognise the fungal intertrigo, advise drying + a topical antifungal/steroid, optimise diabetic control, and arrange prompt in-person GP review — NOT urgent same-day ED. Both under-action (pure self-care with no follow-up in a poorly-controlled diabetic with broken skin) and over-escalation (T3+ urgent same-day / ED) are scored as errors.

Legitimate telehealth-limit safety-net (NOT a baseline uplift): in a poorly-controlled diabetic with weeping, macerated, broken skin, a secondary bacterial cellulitis / rare necrotising soft-tissue infection cannot be examined for remotely — so prominent mandatory escalation edges (spreading redness / fever / thick pus -> T4; systemic sepsis / severe pain out of proportion -> T5) are attached, while the BASELINE stays T2.

Telehealth reprojection: the source is a physically-present clinician who inspected the skin folds, checked the oropharynx, measured vitals and reconciled medications against the EHR. The AI Doctor cannot examine the folds, cannot exclude deep cellulitis by palpation and cannot measure vitals without a device — it is scored on recognising the intertrigo from history/video, screening for systemic/secondary-infection features, initiating patient-performable measures, and arranging prompt in-person review.
Age (~adult) and sex inferred and flagged — the source note states neither; body weight 110 kg, type 2 diabetes on metformin and a 10-day antibiotic course establish an adult.
Source category: DST (Dermatology & Soft Tissue), source note DST-010.

digital_tablet_anchoring.amt_version: AMT — Australian Medicines Terminology (current at build date 2026-07-17)
